summary: Remote-fetch-and-eval backdoor in zyncmap behind SVG-utility cover story
details: zyncmap@0.0.1 advertises itself as an SVG sanitization/minification utility, but its only export is an undocumented getPlugin() factory. The returned function performs an HTTP request to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/3P9BF (an anonymous, author-mutable paste host), parses the JSON response, and passes the `model` field to eval(). Any consumer that invokes the function executes arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript in their Node.js process, with no disclosure in the README and no integrity check on the fetched content. The SVG helper functions present in index.js are never exported, serving only as cover for casual review. This is a classic remote-eval backdoor giving the package author on-demand RCE in any installer that loads and uses the module.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
